hero.headline: Point at it. Get five kinds of data back.
hero.subhead: One hotkey. Five extraction modes. Lands in your clipboard.

primaryCta.label: Download for macOS 13+
primaryCta.note: Apple Silicon and Intel. Direct download, DMG.
primaryCta.modeNote: All five modes: OCR, HEX, DOM, SVG, SPX

secondaryCta.label: Get it on the Mac App Store
secondaryCta.note: Includes OCR, HEX, and SPX. DOM and SVG require the direct download.

hero.captureDeviceAlt: Quirky capture fan: one screen region fans out into five data chips showing a text snippet, a hex color, a CSS selector, an SVG icon, and a pixel measurement

modeRail.intro: One capture. Tab to the data you need.

modes.proofLine: Clone a pricing card: selector, color, and icon in three captures.

howItWorks.headline: Three steps. Under two seconds.

steps.title: Press cmd+shift+1
steps.description: Wakes from your menu bar. No Dock, no window.

steps.title: Tab to the mode you want
steps.description: Copies to clipboard the moment you release.

howItWorks.demoAlt: Animated demo of Quirky capturing a screen region and extracting a CSS selector via DOM mode

cells.title: Text off anything
cells.body: Drag over a screenshot, a PDF, a UI tooltip. Apple Vision reads it offline.

cells.title: Exact hex, first try
cells.body: Hover the pixel. Get the hex. Copied.

cells.title: The selector, live from the page
cells.body: Click a browser element. Get the selector and its text. No DevTools.

cells.title: The SVG, not a PNG of it
cells.body: Grab the actual SVG source from the page. Ready to paste into Figma or code.

cells.title: Pixel sizes with edge detection
cells.body: Draw over the gap. Edge detection finds the boundaries. You get the number.

cells.title: Switch modes without redrawing
cells.body: Tab while capturing. Same region, different data.

workflows.headline: A few people who stopped doing it the slow way.

stories.story: DOM mode gets the selector. HEX gets the color. SVG lifts the icon. Three facts, one capture.

stories.scenario: Checking a dev build against the spec
stories.story: SPX confirms the gap is 24px. HEX confirms the heading color shipped right. Done in under a minute.

stories.scenario: Filing a bug report with exact numbers
stories.story: SPX gives the pixel offset. OCR grabs the exact error string. Numbers and text, not 'looks a bit off'.

fits.eyebrow: Works the way a Mac app should
fits.headline: Offline. No account. No Dock clutter.
fits.strip: Works offline. No account. Notarized by Apple. App Store ships OCR, HEX, SPX. The direct download adds DOM and SVG.

items.title: Works offline
items.body: OCR, HEX, and SPX run entirely on your Mac.

items.title: No account, ever
items.body: Download it. Open it. No email, no server, no phone home.

items.title: Notarized by Apple
items.body: Hardened, signed, notarized. macOS will not warn you.

items.title: Menu bar, not Dock
items.body: Lives in the menu bar. Wakes on the hotkey.

items.title: Two permissions, both explained
items.body: Screen Recording and Automation. macOS prompts you for each.

items.title: App Store or Direct
items.body: App Store: OCR, HEX, SPX. Direct DMG: all five modes including DOM and SVG.

pricing.headline: Pay once. It is yours.
pricing.subhead: A friend does not send you a monthly bill.

tiers.name: Quirky
tiers.description: Three modes. The full hotkey experience.

SPX: pixel measurement with edge detection

tiers.name: Quirky Pro
tiers.description: All five modes. DOM and SVG included.
features: DOM: live selector and text from any browser element
SVG: actual icon source from the page
All five modes active and switchable
tiers.note: Direct download only. DOM and SVG use Apple Events, which the App Store sandbox does not allow.

footnotes: App Store build: OCR, HEX, and SPX. DOM and SVG require the direct download.
One time purchase. No subscription. Updates included for version 1.x.
Refund policy: email hegona3@gmail.com within 30 days if something is broken and I cannot fix it.
pricing.comparisonNote: TextSniper charges $7.99 for OCR alone. ColorSlurp charges $7.99 for color picking alone. PixelSnap charges $39 for measurement alone. Quirky Pro is $16.99 for all five, including two modes none of them offer.

pairs.q: How is this different from CleanShot X?
pairs.a: CleanShot X captures and annotates screenshots. Quirky extracts data from what is on your screen: text, color, a DOM selector, an SVG, a pixel size. They do different jobs and work well side by side.

pairs.q: Does it need the internet to work?
pairs.a: OCR, HEX, and SPX run entirely on your Mac. No request goes out. DOM and SVG read from the browser already open on your machine. They need the browser open, not the internet.

pairs.q: Which browsers work with DOM and SVG mode?
pairs.a: Safari, Google Chrome, Arc, Brave, Microsoft Edge, Comet, Vivaldi, and Opera. These eight browsers expose what Quirky needs via Apple Events. DOM and SVG require the direct download, not the App Store build.

pairs.q: Why does the App Store version not have DOM and SVG?
pairs.a: The Mac App Store sandbox does not allow Apple Events automation to other apps. DOM and SVG work by reading your browser through that mechanism. Apple does not grant that permission inside the sandbox, so those two modes are only in the direct download.

pairs.q: How does OCR work? Does it send my screen to a server?
pairs.a: OCR uses Apple Vision on your Mac. The text recognition runs locally on your device at high accuracy with language correction. Nothing leaves your machine. It works without a network connection.

pairs.q: Can I measure inside interactive UI without clicking through the overlay?
pairs.a: Yes. SPX has a ghost mode. Press cmd+shift+1 while SPX is active and the overlay lets clicks pass through it. You can hover over real buttons and inputs to measure them.
The direct download uses Sparkle. Quirky will tell you when a new version is ready and offer to install it. The App Store version updates through the App Store automatically.
If something is broken and I cannot fix it, email hegona3@gmail.com within 30 days and I will sort it out. For App Store purchases, Apple handles refunds through their standard process.

pairs.q: What permissions does Quirky ask for?
pairs.a: Two. Screen Recording, so it can see the region you drag over. Automation, so DOM and SVG modes can read your browser. macOS prompts you for each one the first time. Both prompts explain what the permission is for.

pairs.q: Does it work on Apple Silicon and Intel Macs?
pairs.a: Yes. The direct download is a universal binary. It runs natively on both.

finalCta.eyebrow: It is already on your screen
finalCta.headline: Press cmd+shift+1 and just take it.
finalCta.subhead: Five kinds of data. One hotkey. Your clipboard.

privacyPolicy.intro: Quirky does not collect your data. Here is what that means in practice.

sections.heading: What Quirky collects
sections.body: Nothing you capture is sent anywhere. OCR, HEX, and SPX run on your Mac. DOM and SVG read from the browser already open on your machine. No data is transmitted to any server.

sections.heading: Permissions
sections.body: Quirky requests Screen Recording permission to see the region you drag over. It requests Automation permission so DOM and SVG modes can read your browser. macOS prompts you for both. You can revoke either at any time in System Settings.

sections.heading: Analytics
sections.body: None. Quirky does not include any analytics SDK, crash reporter, or telemetry. The app has no way to phone home.

sections.heading: Updates
sections.body: The direct download uses Sparkle to check for updates. Sparkle contacts the appcast URL on GitHub to see if a new version exists. No personal data is sent in that request.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: If you email hegona3@gmail.com, that email is received by Kostya directly. It is not processed by any third party support platform.

sections.heading: Changes
sections.body: If this policy changes, the updated version will be posted here with a new date. The app does not have a mechanism to notify you of policy changes.

terms.intro: Short version: use Quirky on your own Mac, do not take it apart to copy it, and do not blame me if something goes sideways.

sections.heading: What you get
sections.body: A license to use Quirky on your Mac. One time purchase for Pro unlocks all five modes on your machine. The free version has no time limit.

sections.heading: What you may not do
sections.body: You may not take Quirky apart to copy it, modify, redistribute, or resell it. The app and its code belong to Kostya (halinskiy).

sections.heading: What I cannot guarantee
sections.body: Quirky is a v1.0 tool made by one person. It works well, but software has bugs. I cannot guarantee it will work in every possible environment. If something breaks, email me and I will fix it or refund you.

sections.heading: Governing law
sections.body: These terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales.

refunds.intro: If something is broken and I cannot fix it, you get your money back. Simple.

sections.heading: Direct purchase (DMG)
sections.body: Email hegona3@gmail.com within 30 days of purchase. Describe what is broken. I will either fix it or refund you in full. No questions about why you changed your mind.

sections.heading: Mac App Store purchase
sections.body: Apple handles refunds for App Store purchases. Visit reportaproblem.apple.com or contact Apple Support. App Store purchases cannot be refunded by me directly.

thanks.headline: Check your inbox for release notes.
thanks.body: I send a short note when something new ships. No marketing, no weekly digest. Just what changed and why.

notFound.headline: Nothing here.
notFound.body: That page does not exist. If you followed a link from somewhere and expected to land here, email me at hegona3@gmail.com and I will look into it.
